title: ledum palustre var. groenlandicum
intro: Ledum palustre var. groenlandicum, also known as Labrador tea, is a type of plant that belongs to the heath family (Ericaceae) and is native to North America. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to act as an anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial agent, making it useful for treating various health issues such as colds, fever, and sore throats. The health benefits of Labrador tea include its potential to reduce stress, improve respiratory health, and boost the immune system. Labrador tea can be prepared as an herbal tea by infusing the leaves in hot water, which is often used as a remedy for various ailments due to its adaptogenic properties.
